skill.name: prompt-repetition
skill.description: A prompt repetition technique for improving LLM accuracy. Achieves significant performance gains in 67% (47/70) of 70 benchmarks. Automatically applied on lightweight models (haiku, flash, mini).
skill.summary: Prompt repetition technique that improves LLM accuracy on lightweight models by 67% in benchmarks.
skill.author: supercent-io

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 52 patterns (external_commands, network, sensitive files) but all are false positives. Backticks are markdown code fences, URLs are academic citations, dictionary .keys() are model identifiers, and 'reasoning' references are Chain-of-Thought patterns. The skill is a legitimate prompt engineering tool with no malicious code.

content.user_title: Boost LLM Accuracy with Prompt Repetition
content.value_statement: Lightweight LLM models often struggle with context-question alignment due to causal attention. This skill automatically repeats prompts to give models a second pass with full context, improving accuracy by up to 76 percentage points.
actual_capabilities: Automatically detects lightweight models (haiku, flash, mini) and applies prompt repetition
Applies 2x repetition for general tasks, 3x for position/index queries
Skips repetition for Chain-of-Thought prompts to avoid conflicts
Monitors token limits and reduces repetitions if context would overflow
Prevents duplicate application with XML markers
limitations: Does not improve reasoning models (Opus, Sonnet, Pro) - already optimized
Not effective with Chain-of-Thought prompting - effects cancel out
Doubles input token usage - may increase costs for very long prompts
Maximum effectiveness is on non-reasoning, lightweight models only

use_cases.title: Multiple Choice Questions with Options First
use_cases.description: When answer choices appear before the question in MCQ tests, repetition helps the model re-read context when answering.
use_cases.target_user: Test preparation developers, educational AI applications

use_cases.title: Position-Based Inventory Queries
use_cases.description: For queries like 'what item is in slot 25?' where position accuracy is critical, 3x repetition dramatically improves accuracy.

use_cases.title: Long Context Information Retrieval
use_cases.description: When searching for specific information in long documents, repetition helps the model better attend to relevant sections.
use_cases.target_user: Document analysis, RAG applications, research assistants

prompt_templates.title: Basic MCQ Format
prompt_templates.scenario: Simple MCQ with options listed first

prompt_templates.title: Position Query Format
Context + Question Format
Auto-Detection Format

output_examples.input:
A. Paris
B. London
C. Berlin

Which city is the capital of France?
output_examples.output: The prompt is automatically repeated twice, giving the model context about the options when processing the question. Expected: Higher accuracy on answering 'A' correctly.

output_examples.input:
Inventory:
1. Iron Sword
2. Leather Armor
...
25. Dragon Scale

What item is in slot 25?
output_examples.output: The prompt is automatically repeated three times. Expected: Dramatic improvement from 21% to 97% accuracy on position queries.

best_practices: Use with lightweight models (haiku, flash, mini) for maximum effect - reasoning models already benefit from their architecture
Check for Chain-of-Thought patterns and disable repetition - CoT and repetition have opposing effects
Monitor token limits and reduce repetitions for long prompts to avoid context overflow
anti_patterns: Applying to reasoning models (Opus, Sonnet, Pro) - wastes tokens with no benefit
Combining with Chain-of-Thought prompts - the techniques interfere with each other
Forcing maximum repetitions on long prompts without checking context limits - causes errors

faq.question: Which models benefit most from prompt repetition?
faq.answer: Lightweight models like Claude Haiku, Gemini Flash, and GPT-4o-mini benefit most. Reasoning models (Opus, Sonnet) already have optimizations that make repetition unnecessary.

faq.question: How much does prompt repetition improve accuracy?
faq.answer: Research shows 67% improvement rate (47/70 benchmarks) with up to 76 percentage point gains on position tasks. No cases of performance degradation were observed.

faq.question: Does prompt repetition increase latency?
faq.answer: Minimal impact. Latency increases only 2-4% because the prefill phase is highly parallelized on GPU. The benefit far outweighs the small delay.

faq.question: Can I use prompt repetition with Chain-of-Thought?
faq.answer: No. Chain-of-Thought and prompt repetition have opposing effects and should not be combined. The skill automatically detects CoT patterns and skips repetition.

faq.question: How do I prevent duplicate application in multi-agent pipelines?
faq.answer: The skill adds an XML marker <!-- prompt-repetition-applied --> to transformed prompts. Check for this marker before applying again.

faq.question: What happens if the repeated prompt exceeds context limits?
faq.answer: The skill automatically reduces repetition count (from 3x to 2x to 1x) to stay within 80% of the model's context window. This prevents errors.
